verdict_reasoning: CRISP1 is a secreted cysteine-rich epididymal protein that becomes stably bound to the sperm surface during epididymal transit. It associates with sperm in a wash-resistant manner (a tightly-bound population persists through capacitation) and plays a role in sperm-egg fusion. This is germline/reproductive-lineage-restricted surface attachment of a secreted protein onto a TM-anchored sperm membrane, fitting stable_surface_attachment in a tissue-restricted context.
